title: Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy - Investments in Training Equipment - Funding program - Canada.ca
content:
## Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy – Investments in Training Equipment

## Changes to Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy - Investments in Training Equipment (CAS-ITE) New
- Shipping and Installation of equipment and materials will now be accepted as eligible expenditures. Please see Glossary , Eligible costs and quote requirements for further details.
- A note to organizations located in Alberta has been added to the Who can apply page and the Prepare to Apply page .
The Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy - Investments in Training Equipment (CAS-ITE) will now accept applications on an ongoing basis.
Funding priorities for the CAS-ITE may change. Prior to applying, check this funding page to ensure that you have the most current and up-to-date information.
Funding decisions will be made on a continuous basis in line with Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC) standard timelines and may take up to 4–6 months.
Application period: Continuous intake Open
Funding amount: Up to $5,000,000 per project for a maximum of 36 months.
Organizations that are located and operate in Canada (except in Quebec), such as:
- Unions representing workers in the Red Seal trades
- Organizations managing training trust funds for unions representing workers in the Red Seal trades
- Training providers that provide technical training to pre-apprentices and apprentices as part of a recognized apprenticeship program for a Red Seal trade
What this funding opportunity offers: Funding for projects that demonstrate a need for trades training equipment and materials that meet industry standards or invest in new technology to train pre-apprentices and apprentices in Red Seal trades.
Note Eligibility consideration: Organizations are required to provide a cash contribution equal to or more than 50% of the cost of new, up-to-date equipment and materials. However, a 30% cash contribution will be accepted if at least 50% of project activities are impacted by challenges such as remote locations, limited infrastructure, or limited partnership opportunities.

## Information sessions
An information session to give an overview of this funding opportunity will be held in fall 2026. Date to be confirmed.
If you would like to register, send an email to EDSC.SCA.EQUIP.MAT-EQUIP.MAT.CAS.ESDC@hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca . Once the session date is confirmed, we will send you instructions on how to attend.
In your email please specify your preferred language (English or French) and if you require any accommodations.

## Help and contact information
For all inquiries, please contact us via the email below.
Email : EDSC.SCA.EQUIP.MAT-EQUIP.MAT.CAS.ESDC@hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca
Accessibility and disability help
If you need help or accommodations, please contact us via the email below.
Email : EDSC.SCA.EQUIP.MAT-EQUIP.MAT.CAS.ESDC@hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
